items.title: Apple now makes one in four iPhones in India: report
items.content: Apple reportedly produced 55 million units in India last year, a shift accelerated as Apple faced growing uncertainty in China.

items.title: An iPhone-hacking toolkit used by Russian spies likely came from U.S military contractor
items.content: Google found a series of hacking tools they said were used by a Russian espionage group and a cybercriminal group in China. Sources from a U.S. government defense contractor s

items.title: Italian prosecutors confirm journalist was hacked with Paragon spyware
items.content: Italian authorities are making progress in their investigation into a wide-ranging spyware scandal in Italy involving Paragon spyware. But the mystery of who hacked two Italia

items.title: MacBook Neo, iPhone 17e, and everything else Apple announced this week
items.content: From a new iPhone to a budget-friendly MacBook, Apple announced a series of new products this week.

items.title: The billion-dollar infrastructure deals powering the AI boom
items.content: Here's everything we know about the biggest AI infrastructure projects, including major spending from Meta, Oracle, Microsoft, Google, and OpenAI.

items.title: The White House wants AI companies to cover rate hikes. Most have already said they would.
items.content: Many hyperscalers have already made public commitments to cover electricity cost increases.

items.title: The public opposition to AI infrastructure is heating up
items.content: Public backlash over the data center boom is leading to a variety of draconian policies — including bans on new construction.

items.title: All the important news from the ongoing India AI Impact Summit
items.content: India is hosting a four-day AI Summit this week that will be attended by executives from major AI labs and Big Tech, including OpenAI, Anthropic, Nvidia, Microsoft, Google, an

items.title: Elevated Energy Prices Add to Fed’s Dilemma on Interest Rates
items.content: Investors now expect that the Fed will delay a rate cut until September instead of July, as they had before the war in Iran began.

items.title: What to Know About the Rising Price of Oil and Its Effects on Airline Ticket Prices
items.content: The Gulf conflict has led to higher fuel prices, and the cost of operating a plane has sharply increased. For travelers, that likely means higher fares.

items.title: YouTube Adds Tool to Help Public Figures Report Fake Videos
items.content: Social media companies are under pressure to crack down on so-called deepfake videos that use deceptive images of real people.

items.title: Rising Fuel Costs Pressure Airlines and Truckers
items.content: The price of jet fuel and diesel has surged since the war in Iran began, which could force airlines and trucking companies to pass on higher costs to their customers.
